# GLOSSARY.md вЂ” Methodology controlled vocabulary (Tensio)

Generated mirror of the methodology's own canon terms вЂ” the framework's
controlled vocabulary that every docstring and generated doc must use
consistently. Terminology drift is invisibility (R-glossary-sync-test).

Source: `spec/src/tensio/glossary.py:TERMS`. Domain-side business terms
(R-ids, axis slugs, stakeholders) live in `spec/content/graph.py` and are
listed in REQUIREMENTS.md / TENSIONS.md вЂ” not duplicated here.

---

## Sections (В§-anchors)
| slug | definition |
|---|---|
| `В§Requirement` | The requirement node вЂ” a claim the system shall satisfy. |
| `В§Conflict` | The first-class connector NODE between requirements, carrying axis+context+steward. |
| `В§Assumption` | A belief with its own lifecycle (HOLDS/UNCERTAIN/DEAD). |
| `В§Axis` | A controlled-vocabulary entry naming a tension dimension. |
| `В§Stakeholder` | Accountability facet: who owns a requirement or stewards a conflict. |
| `В§Invariants` | Structural form of the tension graph (check_* functions). |
| `В§Graph` | The TensionGraph container + traversal + loader. |
| `В§Lifecycle` | The generic state-machine value-type вЂ” keystone for Requirement.status / Conflict.lifecycle / future Operator/Process lifecycles. |
| `В§Operator` | The acting facet of a Stakeholder вЂ” owns a sub-domain, carries a context budget, and runs the closed loop. |
| `В§ContextBudget` | The working-store ceiling of an Operator; the substrate is free. |
| `В§Loop` | The closed loop: Stateв†’Diagnosisв†’Next-actionв†’Actionв†’regenerateв†’State. |
| `В§Glossary` | The methodology's controlled vocabulary (this module). |
| `В§Proposal` | Structured operator-в†’-steward change proposals: the closed loop's ACT half. |
| `В§Closure` | Per-action verify: after apply_proposal writes + regens + pytest-greens, asserts the triggering diagnosis was actually removed. |
| `В§Tick` | The closed-loop diagnostic driver (advisory, M32 conservative): one cycle loads the graph, diagnoses, and emits a TickReport for steward attention. |
| `В§Conscience` | The Hypothesis property-test sweep over the critical-core invariants (M7) вЂ” does my OWN edit introduce a contradiction? |
| `В§Constitution` | The operator's boot sequence вЂ” the generated reconstitution from the substrate's SETTLED laws. |
| `В§Reflection` | The operator self-diagnosis band (P0) вЂ” diagnoses the operator's OWN readiness: DRAFT-overhang, UNENFORCED-SETTLED debt, over-budget operators, dead-assumption-on-enforcer, derived-but-unbuilt. |
| `В§Process` | The opt-in behavioral aspect (M12): a Lifecycle + ordered Steps + roles_required + drives_entities вЂ” the richest contradiction surface. |
| `В§Goal` | A first-class target-state type (M19): distinct from a static Requirement because it carries a MOVING TARGET that yields a Gap driving a Process. |

## Lifecycle states
| slug | definition |
|---|---|
| `DETECTED` | A conflict has been surfaced but no steward action yet. |
| `ACKNOWLEDGED` | Steward accepts the conflict is real and owns it. |
| `DECIDED` | Resolved with recorded rationale and/or a derived requirement. |
| `REVISIT_WHEN` | Parked with an explicit revisit condition (anti-relitigation). |

## Statuses
| slug | definition |
|---|---|
| `DRAFT` | Proposed, not yet accepted into the canon. |
| `SETTLED` | Accepted and currently held. |
| `OPEN` | Accepted-with-a-hole; carries a non-empty question. |
| `REJECTED` | Withdrawn; kept for history (anti-relitigation). |
| `HOLDS` | Assumption currently believed true. |
| `UNCERTAIN` | Assumption whose truth is unconfirmed. |
| `DEAD` | Assumption known false вЂ” dependents are surfaced as DRIFT_FALLOUT. |
| `PROSE` | Enforcement level: recorded only, unenforced (soft context-debt). |
| `STRUCTURAL` | Enforcement level: visible/addressable but no predicate fires. |
| `ENFORCED` | Enforcement level: a check_* invariant or test fires on violation. |

## Roles
| slug | definition |
|---|---|
| `steward` | Holds a conflict; by construction NOT the owner of any of its members. |
| `owner` | Stakeholder who defends a requirement or an assumption. |
| `operator` | An acting agent that owns a bounded sub-domain of the graph. |

## Concepts
| slug | definition |
|---|---|
| `latent connector` | A requirement pair that SHOULD have a Conflict node but doesn't вЂ” a heuristic suspicion. |
| `DRIFT_FALLOUT` | A DEAD assumption with live dependents вЂ” they must be revisited. |
| `crystallize` | Move knowledge from working context into the durable substrate (graph + invariants + docs). |
| `anchor` | A stable, short, typed identifier (R-/C-/A-/В§) the operator references rather than re-carries. |
| `substrate` | The durable, enforced/regenerable/addressable store вЂ” free of context cost. |
| `burn-down` | Promotion of DRAFT requirements to ENFORCED; the methodology's honesty governor. |
| `playbook` | A band-specific procedure the AI operator follows to convert a what_now action into a structured proposal for steward approval. |
| `apply_proposal` | The mechanical writer that lands a steward-approved proposal into spec/content/; the AI never edits the graph by hand. |
| `critical core` | The set of invariants whose violation would silently break the hard boundary or anti-drift вЂ” the conscience's narrow scope (M7). |
| `Step` | One step in a Process вЂ” a verb requiring a declared role (requires_role must be in Process.roles_required). |
| `Gap` | The distance between a Goal's target_state and the current state вЂ” drives Process execution and is the measurable work remaining. |
| `Target state` | The desired-state predicate carried by a Goal (kind in TARGET_KINDS: GRAPH_PROPERTY \| BUSINESS_STATE \| ENTITY_STATE). |
